idea-plugin.name: Gradle Glimpse
idea-plugin.description:
A lifesaver for Flutter developers migrating to AGP 8 and 9.

Updating the Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) in a mature Flutter project often breaks the build due to hidden legacy native dependencies. Gradle Glimpse lives inside your pubspec.yaml to give you instant diagnostic insights.


Features:
  • Project-Wide Diagnostics: Hover over the dependencies: key for a complete AGP upgrade verdict, showing the absolute maximum safe AGP version for your entire project.
  • Instant Compatibility Checks: Hover over any Android-enabled plugin in your pubspec to instantly see its AGP version and compatibility status.
  • Transitive Dependency Tracing: Uncover "hidden" native Android dependencies dragged in by pure Dart packages.
  • AGP 9 Readiness: Automatically detects critical AGP 9 blockers, such as the legacy kotlin-android plugin, missing namespaces, and outdated Java requirements.
  • Flutter Auto-Healing Detection: Intelligently warns you when Flutter is secretly patching legacy code at runtime.
  • Real-Time Syncing: Intelligently watches your file system and instantly updates diagnostics the moment you run flutter pub get.
  • Customizable UI: Toggle the visibility of Glimpse markers at any time via the View menu or right-click context menu.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.1
  • New Feature: Added a project-wide diagnostic summary. Hover over the dependencies: key in your pubspec.yaml for a complete AGP upgrade verdict.
  • New Feature: Added a visibility toggle. You can now hide/show Gradle Glimpse icons via the main View menu or by right-clicking in the editor.
  • Bug Fix: Fixed the "Infinite Cache" bug. Icons and diagnostic data will now instantly update when you save the file or run flutter pub get, without requiring an IDE restart.

1.0.0
  • Initial release: Full AGP compatibility scanning, transitive dependency tracing, and IDE hover UI.

action.description: Toggle the visibility of Gradle Glimpse AGP markers in the sidebar
